title: Turkish Dolma
description: A traditional Turkish dish filled with a mixture of rice, meat, and spices, wrapped in vine leaves. This recipe is a simplified version of the classic dish.
instructions: Rinse the vine leaves and remove the stems.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked rice, ground beef, chopped onion, minced garlic, dolma spice mix, salt, and black pepper.
Place a vine leaf on a flat surface, with the stem end facing you.
Place about 1 tablespoon of the rice mixture in the center of the leaf.
Fold the stem end over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the leaf into a neat package.
Repeat with the remaining leaves and filling.
In a large pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of olive oil.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 30 minutes.
Add the dolmas to the pot and simmer for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until they are tender.
Serve the dolmas hot, garnished with lemon wedges and chopped fresh parsley.
